de.uka.ilkd.key.gui

Class RecentFileMenu

  • java.lang.Object
    • de.uka.ilkd.key.gui.RecentFileMenu

  • public class RecentFileMenu
extends java.lang.Object
    This class offers a mechanism to manage recent files; it adds the necessary menu items to a menu or even can provide that menu itself. also it offers a way to read the recent files from a java.util.Properties object and can store them again to a Properties object. This class is a result of the fusion of the RecentFileBuffer and RecentFiles classes. It's not a Menu in itself!!!

    • Constructor Detail

      • RecentFileMenu

        public RecentFileMenu(KeYMediator mediator)
        Create a new RecentFiles list.
        Parameters:
        listener - the ActionListener that will be notified of the user clicked on a recent file menu entry. The selected filename can be determined with the ActionEvent's getSource() method, cast the Object into a JMenuItem and call the getLabel() method.
        maxNumberOfEntries - the maximal number of items/entries in the recent file menu.
        p - a Properties object containing information about the recent files to be displayed initially. Or null to use no initial information.

    • Method Detail

      • getAbsolutePath

        public java.lang.String getAbsolutePath(javax.swing.JMenuItem item)

      • addRecentFile

        public void addRecentFile(java.lang.String name)
        call this method to add a new file to the beginning of the RecentFiles list. If the name is already part of the list, it will be moved to the first position. No more than a specified maximum number of names will be allowed in the list, and additional names will be removed at the end. (set the maximum number with the setMaxNumberOfEntries(int i) method).
        Parameters:
        name - the name of the file.

      • setMaxNumberOfEntries

        public void setMaxNumberOfEntries(int max)
        specify the maximal number of recent files in the list. The default is MAX_RECENT_FILES

      • getMenu

        public javax.swing.JMenu getMenu()
        the menu where the recent files are kept. If the user didn't specify one in the constructor, a new JMenu is created. It can be accessed via this method.

      • load

        public void load(java.util.Properties p)
        read the recent file names from the properties object. the property names are expected to be "RecentFile0" "RecentFile1" ...

      • store

        public void store(java.util.Properties p)
        Put the names of the recent Files into the properties object. The property names are "RecentFile0" "RecentFile1" ... The values are fully qualified path names.

      • load

        public final void load(java.lang.String filename)
        read the recent files from the given properties file

      • getMostRecent

        public RecentFileMenu.RecentFileEntry getMostRecent()

      • store

        public void store(java.lang.String filename)
        write the recent file names to the given properties file. the file will be read (if it exists) and then re-written so no information will be lost.
